Q8 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Surface imperfection which separates two orientations that are mirror image of one another is called

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Twin boundaries is a special type of grain boundary across which there is specific mirror lattice
symmetry. Twin boundaries occurs in pairs such that the orientation change introduced by one
boundary is restored by the other. The region between the pair of boundaries is called twinned
region. Twinning occurs on a definite crystallographic plane and in a specific direction both of
which depends on crystal structure.

Q19 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct regarding yield point phenomenon?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)
Upper and lower yield points arises due to the presence of interstitial solute atoms like carbon
and nitrogen. These atoms pin down dislocations in the lattice when external stress is applied.
Aluminium and its alloys do not contain significant interstitial solute like carbon and nitrogen
hence yield point phenomenon do not occur.

Q22 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The scanning tunnelling microscope is a powerful instrument usually used to analyze and image at the

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The scanning tunnelling microscope (STM) works on the principle of quantum tunnelling of electron
between a sharp conducting tip and the sample surfaces. It can achieve atomic scale resolution
(even resolving individual atoms) and is widely used for the surface studies in nanotechnology
and material science.

Q25 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The density of a perfect alkali halide having NaCl structure is 2100.3 kg/m3. The density of this alkali halide if it is having 0.16% Frenkel defect will be ______ kg/m3. [Correct upto one decimal place]

NAT
Correct answer: 2100.3
Solution

2100.3 (2100.3 - 2100.3)
There will be no change in density because of Frankel defects, so density with the presence of
frankel defect will be same as density of perfect crystal i.e. ρfrankel = ρperfect = 2100.3 kg/m3.

Q28 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following material combination does not form phase diagram that are completely soluble in liquid state but partially soluble in solid state?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Copper and Nickel are completely soluble in the liquid as well as in solid state.
Ag + Cu and Pb + Sn, there are partially soluble in solid state but fully soluble in liquid state.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct regarding linear imperfections (dislocations) in crystalline solids?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, d)
Line imperfections, or dislocations, in crystalline solids are defects that cause lattice distortion
centered around a line. Dislocations are created during the solidification of crystalline solids.
They are also formed by the permanent or plastic deformation of crystalline solids and by vacancy
condensation and by atomic mismatch in solid solutions.
The two main types of dislocations are the edge and screw types. A combination of the two gives
mixed dislocations, which have edge and screw components. An edge dislocation is created in a
crystal by the insertion of an extra half plane of atoms. The inverted “tee,” ⊥ . indicates a positive
edge dislocation, whereas the upright “tee,” T, indicates a negative edge dislocation.
The displacement distance of the atoms around the dislocation is called the slip or Burgers vector
b and is perpendicular to the edge-dislocation line.
